hero.question: Cincinnati Reds vs. Miami Marlins
hero.prob_take: At 7.5%, the market is pricing the Marlins as a near-impossible underdog, which means you're getting paid almost 13-to-1 odds to back Miami in what is functionally a coinflip sport where any team can win on any given night. The Marlins have been dead money all season, but a 38-point overnight dump on $726K volume looks like retail panic rather than sharp repositioning, leaving value for contrarians willing to fade the consensus.

daily_take.headline: Reds-Marlins Collapse Signals Sharp Money Knows Something You Don't
daily_take.deck: The Reds dropped 38.5 points in 24 hours to hit 7.5%, a bloodbath that smells like pro action. Either Cincinnati's pitching got worse overnight or smart money is pricing in something the public hasn't processed yet. This isn't a fade play, it's a signal to dig into injury reports and Vegas line movement before the book closes.
